MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-23-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WINE.COM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED FACTS SUFFICIENT TO OVERCOME THE PRESUMPTION THAT THE EMPLOYMENT WAS AT WILL. (SEE CAL. LAB. CODE 2922.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THAT THE PARTIES' CONDUCT REVEALED A PROMISE NOT TO DISCHARGE WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE OR FOR A CERTAIN PERIOD OF TIME. (SEE FOLEY V. INTERACTIVE DATA CORP. (1988) 47 CAL.3D 654, 675.) BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DID NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH AN IMPLIED-IN-FACT CONTRACT, NO FACTS HAVE BEEN PLED TO ESTABLISH A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING, AS THAT CAUSE OF ACTION REQUIRES THE EXISTENCE OF A CONTRACT. (WILSON V. 21ST CENTURY INS. CO. (2007) 42 CAL.4TH 713, 720; "THE LAW IMPOSES A COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING IN EVERY CONTRACT.") THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THAT MR. CHOW KNEW THE STATEMENTS WERE FALSE WHEN MADE AND THEREFORE HAS PLED FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THE MALICE REQUIRED TO OVERCOME THE QUALIFIED PRIVILEGE. (COMPLAINT, 31, "CHOW ALSO KNEW THAT THE STATEMENT WAS FALSE.") ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WINE.COM'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SPECIFIC FACTS ALLEGING THAT AN OFFICER OF THE CORPORATION DIRECTED PLAINTIFF TO COMMIT FRAUD, AND AFTER PLAINTIFF REFUSED TO DO SO, HE WAS TERMINATED. THESE FACTS ARE SUFFICIENT TO MEET THE STANDARD OF "OPPRESSION, FRAUD, OR MALICE" SET FORTH IN CAL. CIV. CODE 3294(B). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-30-2015 Set For Trial On Apr-04-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action
Off Calendar Sep-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHEN M. MURPHY, TEL. NO. 1-415-986-1338 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MADONNA A. HERMAN OF THE LAW FIRM OF MANNING & KASS, TEL. NO. 1-415-217-6990 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WINE.COM'S Motion To Dismiss Action is denied. The Stipulation for Settlement (Herman Dec. Ex. A) states "2. To plaintiff for physical injuries induced by emotional distress subject to a 1099." The Court finds that the Stipulation characterized the injuries as physical. The 1099 language does not mandate the issuance of a 1099, , but that it is subject to 1099 treatment only if required. The IRS Form 1099 (Ex. E, Murphy Dec.) appears to indicate that physical injuries do not require issuance of a 1099. Defendant should issue payment accordingly. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2015 Advanced From Feb-17-15 To Feb-17-15 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 212

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Dec-23-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. STEPHEN M. MURPHY, Tel No. (415) 986-1338 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. MADONNA HERMAN of Manning & Kass, Tel No. (415) 217-6990 appearing for the Defendant Wine.com.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WINE.COM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. The court denies summary adjudication as to the wrongful termination in violation of public policy cause of action. Plaintiff's fraud allegations support the wrongful termination claim. See Yau v. Santa Margarita Ford, Inc. (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 144. The court denies summary adjudication as to the defamation cause of action. Whether Mr. Chow stated that he was underwhelmed with plaintiff's team and subsequently painted plaintiff as a liar when he denied the comment is a proveable assertion of fact. That was the issue raised in the moving papers. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Mr. Chow made the false statement. There is also a triable issue of material fact whether he acted maliciously by denying the statement. The court does not entertain defendant's reliance on Coit Drapery Cleaners, Inc. v. Sequoia Ins. Co. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 1595, 1604, because that issue was raised for the first time in the reply brief. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/EHG)

MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WINE.COM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Continued to January 16, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than December 13, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT WINE.COM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action for breach of contract and breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiff has not pled facts sufficient to overcome the presumption that the employment was at will. (See Cal. Lab. Code 2922.) Plaintiff has not pled facts sufficient to establish that the parties' conduct revealed a promise not to discharge without good cause or for a certain period of time. (See Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 675.) Because Plaintiff did not plead facts sufficient to establish an implied-in-fact contract, no facts have been pled to establish a cause of action for breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing, as that cause of action requires the existence of a contract. (Wilson v. 21st Century Ins. Co. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 713, 720; "The law imposes a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract.") The demurrer is overruled as to the fourth cause of action. Plaintiff has pled facts sufficient to establish that Mr. Chow knew the statements were false when made and therefore has pled facts sufficient to establish the malice required to overcome the qualified privilege. (Complaint, 31, "Chow also knew that the statement was false.") ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT WINE.COM'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT is denied. Plaintiff has pled specific facts alleging that an officer of the corporation directed Plaintiff to commit fraud, and after Plaintiff refused to do so, he was terminated. These facts are sufficient to meet the standard of "oppression, fraud, or malice" set forth in Cal. Civ. Code 3294(b). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)
